content: It appears that a few months ago, this line started asserting that the rustc sysroot is a prefix of the rustlib sources. Meanwhile the RUST_LIB_SRC env var still applies although the above change seems to make it useless in practice. I am using nix to get the rust platform and the sources are not contained on the rustc derivation, they are packaged as another derivation so making the script work requires ugly hacks like re-merging the two packages.

Yeah, I also noticed it causes trouble in Ubuntu for the versioned packages (https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/rustc-1.80/+bug/2090791), so I wanted to send an RFC to send a patch to just remove it unless someone thinks it should really be a prefix (and another to change the instructions a bit for Ubuntu). If it is a problem for Nix/you too, then I think it is pretty clear it should go away.
I think it conflicts with the purpose of RUST_LIB_SRC being configurable anyway
Yeah, exactly. I think the point was to avoid mistakes, but at most it should be a warning.
I will Cc you in the RFC -- thanks for this!
